audit_status.source_base: Greek Gospel text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for sea teaching, sower, mystery of the reign, Isaiah hearing/seeing, soils, lamp, measure, seed growing secretly, mustard seed, parables, and storm calming.

verses.restored: Again he began to teach beside the sea. A very great crowd gathered to him, so that he entered a boat and sat in the sea, and the whole crowd was beside the sea on the land.
He taught them many things in parables, and in his teaching he said to them:
Listen. Look, the sower went out to sow.
As he sowed, some fell beside the way, and the birds came and devoured it.
Other seed fell on rocky ground where it did not have much soil, and immediately it sprang up because it had no depth of soil.
When the sun rose, it was scorched; and because it had no root, it withered.
Other seed fell among thorns, and the thorns came up and choked it, and it gave no fruit.
Other seed fell into beautiful/good soil and gave fruit, coming up and increasing; it bore thirty, sixty, and a hundredfold.
He said, Whoever has ears to hear, let that one hear.
When he was alone, those around him with the twelve asked him about the parables.
He said to them, To you has been given the mystery of the reign of the Divine, but to those outside everything comes in parables,
so that seeing they may see and not perceive, and hearing they may hear and not understand, lest they turn back and be released.
He said to them, Do you not know this parable? Then how will you know all the parables?
The sower sows the word.
These are the ones beside the way where the word is sown: when they hear, immediately the adversary comes and takes away the word sown in them.
Likewise, these are the ones sown on rocky ground: when they hear the word, immediately they receive it with joy,
but they have no root in themselves and are temporary. Then when pressure or persecution comes because of the word, immediately they stumble.
Others are the ones sown among thorns: these are those hearing the word,
but the worries of the age, the deceit of wealth, and desires for other things enter in and choke the word, and it becomes fruitless.
Those sown on beautiful/good soil are the ones who hear the word, receive it, and bear fruit: thirty, sixty, and a hundredfold.
He said to them, Does the lamp come so that it may be placed under a basket or under a bed? Is it not to be placed on the lampstand?
For nothing is hidden except to be made visible, nor has anything become concealed except to come into the open.
If anyone has ears to hear, let that one hear.
He said to them, Pay attention to what you hear. With the measure you measure, it will be measured to you, and more will be added to you.
For whoever has, more will be given; and whoever does not have, even what he has will be taken from him.
He said, The reign of the Divine is like this: a person casts seed on the ground,
and sleeps and rises night and day, and the seed sprouts and lengthens; how, he does not know.
The earth bears fruit by itself: first blade, then head, then full grain in the head.
When the fruit is ready, immediately he sends the sickle, because the harvest has come.
He said, How shall we compare the reign of the Divine, or in what parable shall we set it?
It is like a mustard seed, which, when sown on the ground, is smaller than all the seeds on the earth.
Yet when it is sown, it grows up and becomes greater than all the garden plants and makes great branches, so that the birds of the heavens can nest under its shade.
With many such parables he spoke the word to them, as they were able to hear.
He did not speak to them without a parable, but privately to his own disciples he explained all things.
On that day, when evening came, he said to them, Let us pass over to the other side.
Leaving the crowd, they took him along as he was in the boat, and other boats were with him.
A great windstorm arose, and the waves were throwing themselves into the boat, so that the boat was already filling.
He was in the stern, sleeping on the cushion. They woke him and said to him, Teacher, does it not matter to you that we are perishing?
He woke, rebuked the wind, and said to the sea, Be silent; be still. The wind ceased, and there was a great calm.
He said to them, Why are you fearful? Do you not yet have trust?
They feared a great fear and said to one another, Who then is this, that even the wind and the sea obey him?
